rulings.Case Title: ILYA BERIN VS. NEW CENTURY THEATRE SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2019, line 3. DEFENDANT MICHAEL LOPEZ'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant Lopez's motion to vacate the judgment entered on September 24, 2018 is granted. "Code of Civil Procedure section 594, subdivision (a) provides that a trial may not take place unless the adverse party was given at least 15 days' notice. This requirement is mandatory and jurisdictional; a judgment entered following a trial conducted in violation of the requirement is void." (Urethane Foam Experts, Inc. v. Latimer (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 763, 767; accord, Au-Yang v. Barton (1999) 21 Cal.4th 958, 963.) In this case, the court's notice of time and place of trial was not mailed to Lopez's correct address; the court mistakenly served Lopez's former counsel. (Lopez Dec., 3; Lopez's RJN, Ex. 2.) In addition, plaintiff did not provide proper ex parte notice of his application to continue the trial date. Plaintiff mail-served that notice only 2 days before the hearing to the wrong address. Therefore, the record unequivocally establishes that Lopez did not receive proper notice of the trial and the motion must be granted. The parties may contact department 206 to obtain a new trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Lopez is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH WRIGHT VS. AMANDA NOWINSKI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 28, 2019, line 5. DEFENDANT AMANDA NOWINSKI'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant, Cross-Defendant, and Cross-Complainant Nowinski's motion for determination of good faith settlement is denied without prejudice. Nowinski proposes to settle this personal injury case for $1,500. For the Court to find that a settlement is in good faith, the settlors must provide substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of their liability. (See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n. 6; Toyota Motor Sales USA, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 864, 871.) In addition, the court needs to roughly approximate the plaintiff's total recovery to determine whether a settlement is in good faith. (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) In this case, Nowinski presents no evidence that allows the Court to approximate plaintiff's total recovery. There is no evidence regarding plaintiff's injuries, medical bills, lost wages, etc. Plaintiff's $60,000 settlement with co-defendants suggests that the $1,500 proposed settlement may not be in the ballpark, especially since the record demonstrates that Nowinski opened the cab door into plaintiff without looking. (Nowinski Depo., 23:4-6.) This undisputed evidence does not support Nowinski's assertion that her proportionate liability is 0%. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for C&J Leasing, Inc. is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZACHARY BOOKMAN ET AL VS. FRANK NOLAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 28, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT FRANK NOLAN, ANDREW WARREN, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THE ORIGINAL DISCOVERY REQUESTS WERE MADE ON BEHALF OF PLAINTIFF ZACHARY BOOKMAN AND THE UNTIMELY MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES WAS ADDRESSED TO DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES TO THE BOOKMAN REQUESTS. THE SUBSEQUENT DISCOVERY REQUESTS WERE MADE ON BEHALF OF PLAINTIFF DARIAN SHIRAZI. ALTHOUGH IT IS OBVIOUS THAT THE SUBSEQUENT REQUESTS ARE SUBSTANTIALLY IDENTICAL TO THE EARLIER BOOKMAN REQUESTS, EXCEPT FOR SOME CHANGES TO MEET DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO THE EARLIER BOOKMAN REQUESTS, THE COURT CAN FIND NO LEGAL AUTHORITY WHICH WOULD AUTHORIZE IT TO DENY A DIFFERENT PLAINTIFF OF HIS RIGHT TO SEEK DISCOVERY SIMPLY BECAUSE IT SEEKS THE SAME INFORMATION SOUGHT IN AN EARLIER REQUEST BY A DIFFERENT PLAINTIFF, PARTICULARLY WHERE THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES WAS DECIDED BASED ON A FAILURE TO TIMELY FILE RATHER THAN ON THE MERITS. THE TACTICS PURSUED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN THIS CASE MAY WELL BE INCONSISTENT WITH THE INTENT OF THE CALIFORNIA DISCOVERY PROCESS AS EXPRESSED BY THE COURT IN PROFESSIONAL CAREER COLLEGES, MAGNA INSTITUTE, INC. v SUPERIOR COURT, 207CAL.APP.3D 492; NOTHING IN THE HOLDING IN THAT CASE WOULD SUPPORT THE ISSUANCE OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER IN THIS CASE. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST EITHER PARTY. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON MARCH 28, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 28, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JAMAL BISHARAT's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO CERTAIN OF PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS FOLLOWS; WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DEFENDANT WILL PROVIDE, WITHOUT OBJECTION EXCEPT AS TO A CLAIM OF ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE, FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS NOS. 7, 10, 17, 19-24, 32, 39, 41, 43-45, 49, 57 & 63. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT IS WITHHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ON THE BASIS OF A/C PRIVILEGE IT WILL PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH A LOG IDENTIFYING EACH DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT DETAIL TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT AND NONE WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM, AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON MARCH 28, 2019. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)
